label: k-NN & Naive Bayes

node.label: k-NN & Naive Bayes
node.definition: k-nearest-neighbor methods predict from nearby stored examples, while Naive Bayes applies Bayes’ rule under class-conditional feature assumptions.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, k-NN & Naive Bayes is modeled as depending on Datasets, sampling & splits, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Recommendation, ranking & personalization, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
examples: atlas downstream link to Recommendation, ranking & personalization
atlas downstream link to Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: k-NN & Naive Bayes consumes, emits, or organizes information using Datasets, sampling & splits.
In this atlas graph, k-NN & Naive Bayes has a `represents_with` dependency on Embeddings & distributed representations with `common` strength.

In this atlas graph, k-NN & Naive Bayes has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Bayesian & approximate inference because Bayesian & approximate inference is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
k-NN & Naive Bayes follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
